question.text: Under Section 39(7) of the Insurance Act, 1938 (as amended in February 2015), which of the following nominees is classified as a "Beneficial Nominee" who is directly entitled to the insurance proceeds without holding them in trust for legal heirs?

answer.text: A business partner of the policyholder
A friend named in the will of the policyholder

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 39(7) of the Insurance Act, 1938 (amended Feb 2015), a "Beneficial Nominee" is one who is a parent, spouse, or child of the policyholder. Such nominees are beneficially entitled to the insurance proceeds and do not merely hold them in trust for legal heirs. **The Trap:** A sibling is a tempting option because they are a close family member, but siblings are NOT included in the definition of Beneficial Nominee under Section 39(7). Only parents, spouse, and children qualify. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A (Investment Adviser Level 1) also covers nomination concepts. The distinction between nominee and legal heir is tested across multiple NISM certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always advise clients that if they want their insurance proceeds to go directly to a specific person (other than parent/spouse/children), they should create a proper will, as a general nominee only holds the proceeds in trust for legal heirs.
